text:
By Nirmal P. Acharya

The opening ceremony of the Paris Olympic Games was like the closing ceremony of human civilization.

The iconic achievements of human civilization are moving away from the West at a speed that is visible to the naked eye. Today, the West can only pretend to be a beacon of human civilization with “political correctness” such as LGBT.

While a Catholic cardinal called the Paris Olympic opening a “theatre of Satan”, political correctness in the West includes neoliberalism, sanctification of personal interests, high welfare and so on.

To focus on high welfare is actually a trap. When the West successfully disintegrated the Soviet Union and ruthlessly looted and plundered a huge amount of Soviet property and resources, it quickly gave itself a variety of high welfare. These days, these perks go up and they don’t come down. The West will die if it does not continue to loot and pillage others, so the West is targeting China, because only China’s wealth is large enough to feed the US and its small brothers in the West.

Looking back now, China and the US-led West have staged a thrilling “life and death speed”. If China’s power growth is even a step slower, it will be reaped as cruelly as the former Soviet Union. However, when the West was ready to harvest China, it suddenly found that China had transformed into the strongest industrial country and could not harvest China as it had harvested the Soviet Union.

To wage war against China, the world’s most powerful industrial country with 1.4 billion people, 9.6 million square kilometers of land and the centralized leadership of the Communist Party is beyond the scope of experience and imagination of the entire West.

And the West cannot solve its internal crisis if it cannot plunder resources from the outside through war.

Unable to resolve its internal crisis, the West languishes helplessly. This takes the form of running down the road to legalizing drug use, bungee jumping in gender-diverse (up to a hundred) fields, and so on.

Finally, decadence has become a mainstream culture in the West, and it was showcased to great fanfare to the world at the opening ceremony of the Paris Olympics. It was so shocking that the whole world, including the Western world itself, was so shocked that the official website of the Olympic Organizing Committee hurriedly removed the video of the opening ceremony and publicly apologized to the world.

Nepal is an Eastern Hindu country that has made a historic contribution to Eastern culture by establishing Buddhism. However, Nepal has long been colonized by Western spirituality and culture. This is highlighted by the fact that Nepal does not have the same economic foundation as the West, but it has forcibly copied a whole set of Western political systems. It is time to consider saying no to the West and their hypocrisy and double standards.

Nepal has been under the control of the West for more than a hundred years and it must not be easy to say “no” to the West. This can start by saying “no” to the MCC, a gift from the US. Will the sky fall if we return the MCC to the US?

The views expressed in this article are the author’s own and do not necessarily reflect People’s Review’s editorial stance.
